One measured request · one GPU node · decode worker

Basic Kernel Understanding: One-Node Standard Decode

Request-relative CUDA kernel, CUDA graph, and scheduler evidence for a non-speculative GLM-5.2 decode worker.

Contents

Overview Request clock GPU ranks Decode execution tree Kernel timeline Kernel density CUDA graphs Scheduler Kernel families Event explorer Evidence

Overview

This is a structure-first decode trace. It shows which kernels were resident, when the scheduler reported decode work, and which CUDA graph executions can be inferred from the exported Nsight rows.

Primary interpretation
Scope boundary

One Request, Four Clocks

Client attempt is not labeled as the network send. Raw server Receive is t=0; first content, server Finish, and persisted stream EOF are shown separately.

Request completion lane

Admission/transport, first-content, server execution, and stream-drain boundaries.

GPU Rank Processes

Each observed process is labeled with its DP, TP, and EP rank tuple from the node-side PID/GPU mapping. Aggregate residency can exceed wall time because kernels overlap across GPUs and streams.

Device / ranksGPUEventsNamesFirstLastResidencyActive union

Decode Execution Tree: Trace Events to Standard-Decode Modules

The tree assigns every request-window kernel event to graphless work, a repeated full-model standard-decode graph, or an explicitly unlabeled additional graph family. Module labels require stable node order and raw-kernel motifs; capture-local graph IDs alone are never treated as semantics.

Capture-wide phase reconciliation

PhaseScopeKernel eventsAggregate overlap msTrace evidence

Module execution tree

Inclusive subtotal Graphless envelope Standard-decode graph Unassigned graph family Indexer refresh Indexer reuse
    Alignment boundary

    Kernel Event Timeline

    A deterministic browser-sized event view is placed on physical GPU lanes; full-event density and totals remain exact. Communication families use the lower stripe, and the red lines mark first content and server Finish.

    Request-relative launches and intervals

    Hover a visible interval for raw name, launch offset, duration, stream, and graph identity.

    Expanded Kernel Time Density

    Each kernel interval is split across every bin it overlaps. Values are aggregate GPU-ms of kernel residency, not utilization or useful compute.

    Kernel residency per bin

    The dashed baseline is observed GPU count × bin width; same-device stream overlap can exceed it.

    Decode CUDA Graph Executions

    Graph execution spans use CUPTI graph-trace rows when present; graph-node correlation grouping is only the fallback. The graph table and raw API counts expose which evidence path was available.

    Graph execution time and count density

    Bars: aggregate GPU graph span in each bin. Line: graph executions starting in each bin.

    Graph groupExecutionsAggregate spanNode events

    Scheduler Evidence

    Decode rows are timestamped log observations, not a guaranteed one-row-per-token counter. The measured request must have one matched Receive and one matched Finish.

    Scheduler rows, request-relative

    Decode bars use the raw log timestamps; Receive and Finish are distinct lifecycle markers.

    Kernel Families and Raw Names

    Grouping is substring-based and auditable through the raw top names. NCCL and DeepEP are kept separate from model-compute families.

    FamilyEventsWindow residencyRaw event durationAverage overlapTop raw names

    Top kernels by request-window residency

    #FamilyEventsResidencyAverage overlapRaw kernel name

    Kernel Event Explorer

    Filter the deterministic embedded event view by device, family, or raw kernel name. The compressed downloadable CSV retains every analysis-window row.

    #RankLaunchRaw durationWindow overlapFamilyGraphRaw kernel name

    Evidence and Limitations

    The report keeps capture scope, time bases, counts, source artifacts, and graph inference visible so conclusions can be checked without opening Nsight first.

    Interpretation limitKernel residency is interval overlap, not CUDA occupancy, GPU utilization, useful FLOPs, or request latency. Per-GPU residency is summed in aggregate charts.
